Grammatical Profiling for Semantic Change Detection¶

Reproducing Giulianelli, Kutuzov & Pivovarova (2021) on an English historical corpus (1800–1920).

Forschungsfrage: Kann Grammatical Profiling semantischen Wandel englischer Wörter zwischen 1800 und 1920 nachweisen?

Zeitscheiben:

  • T1: 1800–1860
  • T2: 1861–1920

Stichprobenstrategie: Pro Zielwort und Zeitscheibe werden bis zu MAX_OCCURRENCES Token-Vorkommen gesammelt (über alle Dokumente hinweg). Dadurch basieren T1- und T2-Profile auf gleich großen Stichproben.

In [18]:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(__import__('pathlib').Path('..') / 'src'))

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

from semantic_change import (
    Corpus,
    GrammaticalProfiler,
    profile_distance,
    rank_words,
    plot_profiles,
    plot_heatmap,
    plot_ranking,
)
from semantic_change.visualization import plot_feature_diff

%matplotlib inline
plt.rcParams['figure.dpi'] = 120

# --- Parameter ---
MAX_OCCURRENCES = 50   # Token-Vorkommen pro Wort und Zeitscheibe
SEED = 42              # Zufalls-Seed (None = nicht reproduzierbar)
SAVE_PLOTS = True      # Plots als PNG speichern?
SAVE_TABLES = True     # Tabellen als CSV speichern?
OUTPUT_DIR = Path("../output")

if SAVE_PLOTS or SAVE_TABLES:
    OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

def save(fig: plt.Figure, name: str) -> None:
    if SAVE_PLOTS:
        path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{name}.png"
        fig.savefig(path, dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
        print(f"  → gespeichert: {path}")

def save_csv(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str) -> None:
    if SAVE_TABLES:
        path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{name}.csv"
        df.to_csv(path)
        print(f"  → gespeichert: {path}")

1 Korpus laden¶

In [4]:
# Alle verfügbaren Dokumente laden (kein Datei-Limit).
# Das Profiling selbst stoppt pro Wort, sobald MAX_OCCURRENCES erreicht sind.
corpus = Corpus(
    t1_range=(1800, 1860),
    t2_range=(1861, 1920),
)

texts = corpus.load()
print("Token-Schätzung:", corpus.token_counts())

[Corpus] T1 (1800–1860): loaded 4739 files, 0 not found.
[Corpus] T2 (1861–1920): loaded 21698 files, 2 not found.
Token-Schätzung: {'T1': 88984533, 'T2': 291199757}
In [5]:
# Korpus-Überblick: Dateien pro Jahr
stats = corpus.stats()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 4))
for label, grp in stats.groupby('slice'):
    ax.bar(grp['year'], grp['n_files'], label=label, alpha=0.7, width=1)
ax.set_xlabel('Jahr')
ax.set_ylabel('Anzahl Dateien')
ax.set_title('Korpus-Verteilung über die Zeit')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
save(fig, "corpus_distribution")
plt.show()

  → gespeichert: ../output/corpus_distribution.png

2 Grammatical Profiling¶

Das Profiling durchläuft die Dokumente jeder Zeitscheibe sequenziell. Sobald für ein Wort MAX_OCCURRENCES Token gesammelt wurden, wird es übersprungen. Dadurch ist die Stichprobengröße für T1 und T2 je Wort gleich.

In [6]:
TARGET_WORDS = [
    "steam", "electric", "race", "progress", "science", "machine",
    "labour", "industry", "telegraph", "evolution",
    "empire", "reform", "capital", "engine",
]

profiler = GrammaticalProfiler(
    target_words=TARGET_WORDS,
    feature_type="both",   # dep + morph
    min_freq_ratio=0.05,
)

profiles = profiler.build_profiles(texts, max_occurrences=MAX_OCCURRENCES, seed=SEED)
print("\nProfiling abgeschlossen.")

[Profiler] Loading spaCy model 'en_core_web_sm' …

3 Stichprobenbalance prüfen¶

Wörter mit weniger als MAX_OCCURRENCES Funden in einer Zeitscheibe sollten mit Vorsicht interpretiert werden.

In [7]:
import numpy as np

balance_rows = []
for word in TARGET_WORDS:
    for label in ("T1", "T2"):
        profile = profiles.get(label, {}).get(word)
        # Total feature counts ≈ number of occurrences sampled
        n = sum(profile.values()) if profile else 0
        balance_rows.append({"word": word, "slice": label, "n_features": n})

balance_df = pd.DataFrame(balance_rows)

pivot = balance_df.pivot(index="word", columns="slice", values="n_features").fillna(0).astype(int)
pivot["ratio T2/T1"] = (pivot["T2"] / pivot["T1"].replace(0, np.nan)).round(2)
pivot.sort_values("ratio T2/T1", ascending=False)
Out[7]:
slice T1 T2 ratio T2/T1
word
capital 82 96 1.17
electric 62 69 1.11
telegraph 72 78 1.08
machine 91 91 1.00
science 91 90 0.99
reform 67 66 0.99
race 91 89 0.98
industry 92 90 0.98
evolution 94 91 0.97
labour 76 74 0.97
steam 78 73 0.94
engine 89 84 0.94
empire 97 88 0.91
progress 98 81 0.83
In [8]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
x = np.arange(len(TARGET_WORDS))
w = 0.35
for i, label in enumerate(["T1", "T2"]):
    vals = [sum(profiles.get(label, {}).get(word, {}).values()) for word in TARGET_WORDS]
    ax.bar(x + (i - 0.5) * w, vals, w, label=label, alpha=0.8)
ax.axhline(MAX_OCCURRENCES, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, label=f'Ziel ({MAX_OCCURRENCES})')
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(TARGET_WORDS, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.set_ylabel('Feature-Einträge')
ax.set_title('Stichprobenbalance: T1 vs T2 pro Wort')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
save(fig, "sample_balance")
plt.show()

  → gespeichert: ../output/sample_balance.png

5 Heatmap: Distanzen nach Feature-Kategorie¶

In [11]:

save(fig, "heatmap")

6 Detailanalyse einzelner Wörter¶

In [12]:
# Zielwort für die Detailansicht wählen
WORD = "machine"

p1 = profiles.get("T1", {}).get(WORD)
p2 = profiles.get("T2", {}).get(WORD)

if p1 and p2:
    dist = profile_distance(p1, p2)
    print(f"Cosinus-Distanz für '{WORD}': {dist:.4f}")
    fig = plot_profiles(WORD, p1, p2)
    save(fig, f"profile_{WORD}")
    plt.show()
else:
    print(f"Kein Profil für '{WORD}' gefunden (zu selten im Korpus?).")

Cosinus-Distanz für 'machine': 0.0296
  → gespeichert: ../output/profile_machine.png
In [13]:
# Feature-Shift: welche Eigenschaften haben sich am stärksten verändert?
if p1 and p2:
    fig = plot_feature_diff(WORD, p1, p2)
    save(fig, f"feature_diff_{WORD}")
    plt.show()

  → gespeichert: ../output/feature_diff_machine.png

7 Alle Zielwörter im Überblick¶

In [14]:
from collections import Counter

words_with_profiles = [
    w for w in TARGET_WORDS
    if profiles.get("T1", {}).get(w) and profiles.get("T2", {}).get(w)
]

n_cols = 3
n_rows = -(-len(words_with_profiles) // n_cols)  # ceiling division
fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(18, n_rows * 4))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, word in enumerate(words_with_profiles):
    p1 = profiles["T1"][word]
    p2 = profiles["T2"][word]
    all_keys = sorted(set(p1) | set(p2))

    def norm(c):
        t = sum(c.values()) or 1
        return {k: c.get(k, 0) / t for k in all_keys}

    n1, n2 = norm(p1), norm(p2)
    combined = sorted(all_keys, key=lambda k: n1[k] + n2[k], reverse=True)[:10]

    x = np.arange(len(combined))
    w = 0.35
    axes[i].bar(x - w/2, [n1[k] for k in combined], w, label="T1", alpha=0.8)
    axes[i].bar(x + w/2, [n2[k] for k in combined], w, label="T2", alpha=0.8)
    axes[i].set_xticks(x)
    axes[i].set_xticklabels(combined, rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=7)
    d = profile_distance(p1, p2)
    axes[i].set_title(f"{word}  (d={d:.3f})", fontsize=10)
    axes[i].legend(fontsize=7)

for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)

fig.suptitle("Grammatical Profiles: T1 (1800–1860) vs T2 (1861–1920)", fontsize=13, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
save(fig, "all_profiles")
plt.show()

  → gespeichert: ../output/all_profiles.png

8 Nur Dependency Relations (stärkster Prädiktor für Englisch)¶

In [15]:
profiler_dep = GrammaticalProfiler(
    target_words=TARGET_WORDS,
    feature_type="dep",
    min_freq_ratio=0.05,
)

profiles_dep = profiler_dep.build_profiles(texts, max_occurrences=MAX_OCCURRENCES, seed=SEED)
ranking_dep = rank_words(profiles_dep, slice_labels=("T1", "T2"))

fig = plot_ranking(
    ranking_dep,
    title="Ranking (nur Dependency Relations)",
    color="#e07b39",
)
save(fig, "ranking_dep")
plt.show()

9 Methodenvergleich: Morphologie vs. Syntax¶

In [19]:
dep_cols = [c for c in ranking.columns if c == 'dep']
morph_cols = [c for c in ranking.columns if c not in ('word', 'distance', 'dep')]

if dep_cols and morph_cols:
    comp = ranking[['word', 'distance'] + dep_cols + morph_cols].copy()
    comp['morph_mean'] = comp[morph_cols].mean(axis=1)

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 5))
    ax.scatter(comp['dep'], comp['morph_mean'], s=80, alpha=0.8)
    for _, row in comp.iterrows():
        ax.annotate(row['word'], (row['dep'], row['morph_mean']), fontsize=8,
                    xytext=(4, 4), textcoords='offset points')
    ax.set_xlabel("Dependency distance")
    ax.set_ylabel("Mean morphology distance")
    ax.set_title("Syntaktischer vs. morphologischer Wandel")
    plt.tight_layout()
    save(fig, "morph_vs_dep")
    plt.show()
else:
    print("Nicht genug Kategorien für Vergleich.")

  → gespeichert: ../output/morph_vs_dep.png

Tabelle 1: Korpus-Überblick¶

In [26]:
token_counts = corpus.token_counts()
stats_summary = corpus.stats()

corpus_table = pd.DataFrame([
    {
        "Zeitscheibe": label,
        "Jahre": f"{start}–{end}",
        "Dokumente (N)": stats_summary[stats_summary["slice"] == label]["n_files"].sum(),
        "Token (geschätzt)": f"{token_counts[label]:,}",
    }
    for label, (start, end) in corpus.slices.items()
])
corpus_table = corpus_table.set_index("Zeitscheibe")

save_csv(corpus_table, "tab1_korpus")
corpus_table

  → gespeichert: ../output/tab1_korpus.csv

Tabelle 2: Hauptergebnis – Cosinus-Distanzen (T1 vs. T2)¶

In [27]:
# Haupttabelle: Rang, Wort, Gesamtdistanz, Distanz nach Kategorie
result_table = ranking.copy()
result_table.index = result_table.index + 1  # Rang ab 1
result_table.index.name = "Rang"
result_table["distance"] = result_table["distance"].round(4)

# Kategoriespalten runden
cat_cols = [c for c in result_table.columns if c not in ("word", "distance")]
result_table[cat_cols] = result_table[cat_cols].round(4)

result_table = result_table.rename(columns={"word": "Wort", "distance": "Cosinus-Distanz"})

save_csv(result_table, "tab2_hauptergebnis")
result_table

  → gespeichert: ../output/tab2_hauptergebnis.csv

Tabelle 3: Top-5-Features pro Wort (T1 und T2 getrennt)¶

In [28]:
# Für jedes Wort: die 5 häufigsten grammatischen Merkmale in T1 und T2 (relative Frequenz)
rows = []
for word in TARGET_WORDS:
    for label in ("T1", "T2"):
        profile = profiles.get(label, {}).get(word)
        if not profile:
            continue
        total = sum(profile.values())
        for feat, count in profile.most_common(5):
            rows.append({
                "Wort": word,
                "Zeitscheibe": label,
                "Merkmal": feat,
                "rel. Freq.": round(count / total, 3),
            })

top_features = pd.DataFrame(rows)
save_csv(top_features, "tab3_top_features")
top_features

  → gespeichert: ../output/tab3_top_features.csv
Out[28]:
Wort Zeitscheibe Merkmal rel. Freq.
0 steam T1 Number:Sing 0.538
1 steam T1 dep:compound 0.397
2 steam T1 dep:pobj 0.064
3 steam T2 Number:Sing 0.493
4 steam T2 dep:compound 0.274
... ... ... ... ...
112 engine T2 Number:Sing 0.393
113 engine T2 Number:Plur 0.202
114 engine T2 dep:pobj 0.179
115 engine T2 dep:nsubj 0.131
116 engine T2 dep:compound 0.095

117 rows × 4 columns

Tabelle 4: Größte Feature-Shifts pro Wort\n\nWelche grammatischen Merkmale haben sich für jedes Wort am stärksten verändert? (Δ = rel. Freq. T2 − T1)¶

In [29]:
# Für jedes Wort: das Merkmal mit der größten absoluten Verschiebung
rows = []
for word in TARGET_WORDS:
    p1 = profiles.get("T1", {}).get(word)
    p2 = profiles.get("T2", {}).get(word)
    if not p1 or not p2:
        continue

    all_keys = set(p1) | set(p2)
    t1_total = sum(p1.values()) or 1
    t2_total = sum(p2.values()) or 1
    n1 = {k: p1.get(k, 0) / t1_total for k in all_keys}
    n2 = {k: p2.get(k, 0) / t2_total for k in all_keys}

    diffs = {k: round(n2[k] - n1[k], 4) for k in all_keys}
    top3 = sorted(diffs, key=lambda k: abs(diffs[k]), reverse=True)[:3]

    for feat in top3:
        rows.append({
            "Wort": word,
            "Merkmal": feat,
            "Δ (T2−T1)": diffs[feat],
            "T1": round(n1[feat], 4),
            "T2": round(n2[feat], 4),
            "Richtung": "↑ häufiger" if diffs[feat] > 0 else "↓ seltener",
        })

shifts_table = pd.DataFrame(rows)
save_csv(shifts_table, "tab4_feature_shifts")
shifts_table

  → gespeichert: ../output/tab4_feature_shifts.csv
Out[29]:
Wort Merkmal Δ (T2−T1) T1 T2 Richtung

Tabelle 5: Deskriptive Statistiken der Cosinus-Distanzen¶

In [30]:
# Deskriptive Statistiken über alle Zielwörter
desc = ranking["distance"].describe().rename({
    "count": "N",
    "mean":  "Mittelwert",
    "std":   "Std.-Abw.",
    "min":   "Min.",
    "25%":   "Q1",
    "50%":   "Median",
    "75%":   "Q3",
    "max":   "Max.",
}).round(4).to_frame(name="Cosinus-Distanz")

# Wort mit stärkstem / schwächstem Wandel
desc.loc["stärkster Wandel"] = ranking.loc[0, "word"]
desc.loc["schwächster Wandel"] = ranking.iloc[-1]["word"]

save_csv(desc, "tab5_deskriptiv")
desc

  → gespeichert: ../output/tab5_deskriptiv.csv
